reasoning: The Lean statement universally quantifies over groups G, assumes G/Z(G) is cyclic via `IsCyclic (G ⧸ center G)`, and concludes `center G = ⊤`. The conclusion `center G = ⊤` (the center is the whole group) is exactly "G = Z", which is logically equivalent to "G is abelian". The informal statement's two conclusions ("G is abelian" AND "G = Z") are equivalent to each other and both captured by `center G = ⊤`. The center is normal in Mathlib (instNormalCenter), so the quotient group is well-defined. This is a faithful formalization.
